framework.name: AP2 Trust Model: Allow-lists now → mTLS/DNSSEC later
framework.description: AP2 starts with curated allow-lists of trusted participants (agents, credential providers, merchants) and plans to progress to real-time identity assurance using HTTPS, DNS/DNSSEC ownership, and mutual TLS. Pragmatic staged rollout.
framework.raw_quote: Trust model is anchored today by signed mandates and curated allow-lists of trusted participants. Over time, AP2 will progress toward real-time identity assurance using open internet standards such as HTTPS, DNS ownership, and mutual TLS.

source.title: PayPal Community Blog | Agent Payments Protocol: Building Verifiable Trust for Agentic Commerce
